name: Soto Ayam (Chicken Soup)
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove chicken giblets and set aside.
2. Pull off and discard any lumps of fat from the chicken.
3. Cut off wings and leg-and-thigh pieces. Separate back from breast.
4. Heat oil in a 6-quart kettle over medium heat.
5. Add garlic, onion, and ginger; cook, stirring until onion is soft.
6. Add lemongrass, galangal, turmeric, salt, coriander, sugar, pepper, broth, and water to the pot.
7. Stir then add chicken and giblets (excluding liver). Bring to a boil over high heat.
8. Cover, reduce heat, and simmer until breast is no longer pink when slashed (15-20 minutes).
9. Remove breast and let cool. Simmer remaining chicken until meat near thighbone is no longer pink when slashed (15 more minutes).
10. Lift out legs and thighs; let cool.
11. Continue to simmer broth. Discard skin from breast, legs, and thighs.
12. Cut meat into bite-sized pieces. Cover and set aside.
13. Return bones to the broth; continue simmering until broth is richly flavored (about 1 more hour).
14. Strain the broth, discard wings, back, bones, giblets, and seasonings.
15. At this point, you may cover and refrigerate chicken and broth until next day. Bring chicken to room temperature before serving.
16. Prepare condiments and crisp-fried onions in separate bowls.
17. Skim and discard fat from the broth.
18. Heat broth until steaming; stir in lemon juice.
19. Ladle broth into serving bowls. Let diners add chicken, condiments, and crisp-fried onions.
Condiments:
Offer at least two of the following: 2 ounces bean threads (soaked in warm water for 30 minutes before cutting), or 1.5 cups cooked rice; 1.5 cups bean sprouts mixed with 1/4 cup chopped celery leaves; Spiced Coconut; and Chili Paste.
Crisp-fried onions:
1. Cut 2 medium onions lengthwise into halves.
2. Cut each half crosswise into even slices about 1/8 inch thick.
3. Further cut slices into halves or thirds to make smaller arcs.
4. In a deep, heavy pan, pour salad oil to a depth of 1 inch and heat to 300°F.
5. Add about one-third of the onions and cook, stirring often until lightly browned (4-5 minutes). The oil temperature will drop initially but rise again as the onions brown; maintain 300°F by adjusting heat.
6. Remove onions with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
7. Repeat with remaining onions.
8. Serve warm or at room temperature. Makes about 1.5 cups.
